title: Paul's Third Missionary Journey
description: A teaching route for Paul's later ministry with emphasis on Ephesus, Macedonia, and the return to Jerusalem.
caution: This route combines Acts' travel narrative with later epistolary context. It is a concise teaching map, not a complete itinerary.

stops.name: Antioch in Syria
stops.description: The journey begins again in Antioch.
stops.notes: Starting point after a return from prior ministry.

stops.name: Ephesus
stops.description: The long teaching ministry in Ephesus.
stops.notes: A key center for the third journey.

stops.name: Troas
stops.description: A brief but important stop on the way west.
stops.notes: The text gives this stop special attention.

stops.name: Philippi
stops.notes: A familiar city from the second journey.

stops.name: Corinth
stops.description: A major pastoral and correspondence center.
stops.notes: Letters and Acts both make this a significant waypoint.

stops.name: Miletus
stops.description: The farewell speech to the Ephesian elders.

stops.name: Jerusalem
stops.notes: A secure final stop in this teaching outline.

segments.label: Strengthening the churches
segments.description: Paul travels to Ephesus and establishes a long ministry there.

segments.label: Toward the coast
segments.caution: A direct line compresses multiple steps.

segments.label: South to Greece
segments.description: The route swings south toward Corinth.
segments.caution: This leg combines several regional moves.

segments.label: Farewell route
segments.description: Paul heads to Miletus for the farewell speech.

segments.label: Return to Jerusalem
segments.caution: Acts summarizes the final travel sequence.
